title: Add `symphony info` command to print environment diagnostics
description:
## Summary

Add a new `symphony info` (alias `sy info`) subcommand that prints basic environment diagnostics. This is a self-contained, low-risk command intended partly as a Symphony end-to-end demo.

The command must implement three clearly distinct feature points:

### 1. Version & platform line

Print the Symphony version (from `symphony.__version__`) together with the running Python version and the OS platform, e.g.:

```
Symphony 0.x.y — Python 3.12.3 on darwin
```

### 2. Workflow summary

Resolve the workflow file (default `./WORKFLOW.md`, overridable with a positional `workflow_path` argument like the main CLI). Parse it and print:

* the resolved absolute workflow path
* the configured agent runner (`agent.runner`)
* the tracker kind (`tracker.kind`)

If no workflow file is found, print a clear `not found` line for these fields instead of crashing.

### 3. `--json` flag

Support a `--json` flag that emits the exact same information as a single JSON object (keys: `version`, `python`, `platform`, `workflow_path`, `runner`, `tracker`) instead of the human-readable text. Without the flag, keep the readable multi-line text output.

## Acceptance criteria

* `symphony info` prints the three sections in human-readable form and exits 0.
* `symphony info --json` prints valid JSON with the six keys and exits 0.
* Missing `WORKFLOW.md` does not crash either mode.
* Add a focused unit/integration test covering both the text and `--json` paths.

## Out of scope

* No changes to the orchestrator, polling, or dispatch logic.
* No network calls.
